project.name: Fluxbot Studio IA
project.description: Shopify AI Chatbot App — Conversational Commerce Platform

phase0.title: Foundation regression contract (auth / navigation / env / build / Shopify connection)
phase0.description: Core Phase 0 test suite that must remain green on every production change
acceptance_criteria: 68/68 Phase 0 tests passing
JWT HS256 signature validated
shop/host/embedded query params preserved across navigation
Shopify Admin GraphQL connection verified
Required environment variables validated
TypeScript build and project structure validated

phase0.title: Frontend configuration decoupled from provider secrets
phase0.description: Config layer reads IA_BACKEND_URL / IA_BACKEND_API_KEY; provider API keys live only in backend repo
acceptance_criteria: No provider API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) in frontend .env
IA_BACKEND_URL and IA_BACKEND_API_KEY are the only IA-related env vars
Local fallback available via IA_EXECUTION_MODE=local for development only

phase1.title: Shopify embedded app shell + admin routes
phase1.description: Full embedded admin with Polaris UI, App Bridge, OAuth and all admin pages
acceptance_criteria: App loads embedded in Shopify admin
All admin pages render without errors
OAuth flow completes and session is persisted
Polaris layout applied consistently

phase1.title: HTTP gateway and contract toward fluxbot-studio-back-ia
phase1.description: Typed client and server-side gateway that routes all IA calls to the backend repo over versioned /api/v1/* contracts
acceptance_criteria: All IA calls go through IAGateway abstraction
Contract tests validate request/response shapes
Remote-first by default, local fallback for development
Versioned endpoints (/api/v1/*) used consistently

phase1.title: Chat orchestration and retrieval migrated to backend IA
phase1.description: api.chat.ts delegates to backend via gateway; no LLM calls in frontend
acceptance_criteria: Chat route delegates to IAGateway, no direct LLM calls in frontend
Backend orchestrates provider selection, retrieval and generation
Guardrails applied in backend

phase1.title: Order lookup — read-only commerce capability
phase1.description: Customers can query order status; frontend owns the lookup, backend IA can invoke it as a tool
acceptance_criteria: Order status returned for verified order reference
No mutation of order data
Route secured; PII not leaked

phase1.title: PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM — multi-tenant schema
phase1.description: Full schema deployed; all frontend entities with tenant isolation
acceptance_criteria: All migrations deployed
shopId foreign key on all tenant-scoped models
Prisma client generated and typed

phase2.title: Behavioral event capture and conversion signal storage
phase2.description: Frontend captures browser events (dwell, scroll, exit intent, cart, product view) and stores them for analysis
acceptance_criteria: Single and batch event endpoints working
BehaviorEvent model persisted in DB
Events forwarded as inputs to backend IA for analysis

phase2.title: Intent detection and trigger decisioning — migrated to backend IA
phase2.description: Intent classification and trigger evaluation are remote-only; frontend only proxies via gateway
acceptance_criteria: Intent analysis route delegates to /api/v1/intent/* contract
Trigger evaluation delegates to /api/v1/triggers/*
No local fallback — backend IA is required
Contract tests pass

phase2.title: Commerce execution layer (add-to-cart, handoff persistence)
phase2.description: Frontend owns cart mutation and handoff record; backend IA can invoke these as tools
acceptance_criteria: Cart add action validated and executed via Shopify Storefront API
HandoffRequest record persisted with full conversation context
Both routes secured and audited

phase2.title: Proactive messaging — decisioning in backend, dispatch in frontend
phase2.description: Backend IA decides when and what to send; frontend dispatches and tracks cooldowns
acceptance_criteria: Proactive trigger evaluation proxied to backend
Dispatch and frequency capping handled in frontend
ProactiveMessage records persisted

phase2.title: Advanced reranking and RAG quality pipeline
phase2.description: RAG search delegated to backend via versioned contract with quality params (minScore, rerankStrategy, topK)
acceptance_criteria: RAG search uses /api/v1/rag/search with quality params
Reranking strategy configurable per request
Quality metadata returned and logged

phase3.title: Omnichannel delivery and callback operations
phase3.description: WhatsApp, Instagram, SMS, Email delivery surfaces with callback handling
acceptance_criteria: Delivery callback route handles external channel acknowledgements
DeadLetterCallback model captures failed deliveries
OmnichannelCallbackReceipt persisted per delivery
phase3.next_step: Add provider-specific onboarding (WhatsApp Business API, Instagram DM) and merchant controls in admin UI

phase3.title: llms.txt generation and publication
phase3.description: Backend generates structured llms.txt content; frontend publishes it at the public endpoint
acceptance_criteria: GET /llms.txt returns valid structured content
Admin page shows generation status
Backend generation contract stable and versioned

phase3.title: Marketing automations and multilingual campaigns
phase3.description: Campaign CRUD, dispatch and multilingual template resolution; backend IA emits campaign metadata in trigger payloads
acceptance_criteria: Campaign CRUD endpoints working
Dispatch endpoint triggers proactive message delivery
Localization service resolves templates by locale
Backend emits campaignId in trigger recommendation payloads

phase4.title: Enterprise compliance and governance
phase4.description: Data residency, regional deployment controls, retention enforcement, legal holds, breach notifications, RGPD audit trail
acceptance_criteria: DataResidencyService enforces country-level storage rules
RegionalDeploymentControlService configures per-region controls
LegalHoldService creates, lists and releases holds
ProcessingRecordService tracks RGPD activity register
BreachNotificationService tracks 72h reporting window
RetentionEnforcementService deletes data beyond retention thresholds
Scheduler job enforces retention policies periodically

phase5.title: Enterprise connector hardening (SIEM, legal hold workflow, regional deployment)
phase5.description: Extended governance infrastructure for enterprise merchants
acceptance_criteria: Legal hold and deployment policy endpoints operational
Retention exclusions scoped per data class
phase5.next_step: Integrate external SIEM adapters (Datadog/Splunk) and persist legal holds in DB

phase6.title: Architecture separation closure
phase6.description: All IA logic routed through IAGateway; legacy local IA services preserved only as controlled fallback
acceptance_criteria: Primary chat and retrieval flows use remote gateway
Local compatibility path only active with IA_EXECUTION_MODE=local
Contract-first gateway tests passing
SEPARATION_PLAN.md documents all migrated capabilities

phase7.title: Storefront widget publication admin surface
phase7.description: Admin UI for publishing and managing the storefront chat widget via Theme App Embed
acceptance_criteria: Widget publish/reset controls working in admin
Widget config served via App Proxy
Storefront widget routes (apps.fluxbot.*) accessible
phase7.next_step: Replace manual confirmation with automatic Theme App Embed verification via Shopify Admin API

phase8.title: Remote vector retrieval contract (/api/v1/embeddings/search)
phase8.description: Frontend vector retrieval service uses backend IA as remote-first with local JSON fallback for development
acceptance_criteria: Vector retrieval proxied to /api/v1/embeddings/search
Contract test validates request/response shape
Local JSON fallback only when IA_EXECUTION_MODE=local
phase8.next_step: Migrate storage from JSON embeddings to pgvector or ANN index for production-scale retrieval

backend_ia.title: Express API server with authentication and rate limiting
backend_ia.description: Backend IA entrypoint with JWT auth middleware, helmet, cors, rate limiter and structured logging
acceptance_criteria: API starts and accepts requests
JWT auth middleware validates IA_BACKEND_API_KEY
Rate limiting active
Structured logs emitted via winston

backend_ia.title: LLM orchestration — provider-agnostic chat
backend_ia.description: Orchestration service selects provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini), composes context and generates responses
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/chat accepts message + conversationId + context
Provider selected based on AIProviderConfig for the shop
Guardrails applied before response is returned

backend_ia.title: RAG — retrieval augmented generation pipeline
backend_ia.description: Hybrid search with embeddings + metadata filters + optional reranking
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/rag/search returns ranked chunks
Quality params (minScore, rerankStrategy, topK) supported
KnowledgeChunk and EmbeddingRecord models in DB
POST /api/v1/embeddings/search for vector retrieval

backend_ia.title: Intent detection and trigger decisioning
backend_ia.description: Intent classifier and proactive trigger evaluation fully owned by backend IA
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/intent/analyze returns intent classification
POST /api/v1/triggers/evaluate returns trigger recommendation
IntentSignal and ProactiveTrigger persisted in DB

backend_ia.title: Knowledge graph, commerce tools and analytics
backend_ia.description: Knowledge graph for product relationships, commerce tool service and IA analytics tracking
acceptance_criteria: Knowledge graph routes available
Commerce tool service returns structured product/policy data
IA analytics (tokens, cost, latency) tracked per conversation

backend_ia.title: Shop reference sync and AI provider config management
backend_ia.description: Backend maintains Shop reference table and per-shop AI provider configuration
acceptance_criteria: Shop reference created/updated on first sync from frontend
AIProviderConfig managed per shop with encrypted API keys
Provider selection respects per-shop config

backend_ia.title: llms.txt content generation
backend_ia.description: Backend generates structured llms.txt content; frontend publishes it
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/llms-txt/:shopId returns structured content
Content includes product summaries, policies and FAQs
Frontend publication route calls this endpoint

backend_ia.title: Enterprise compliance and omnichannel routes in backend
backend_ia.description: Backend-side compliance checks and omnichannel delivery support
acceptance_criteria: Compliance route exposes data residency and governance APIs
Omnichannel route supports multi-channel delivery decisions

open_items.title: Storefront widget — Theme App Extension (real embed)
open_items.description: Actual Liquid/JS storefront widget installable via Theme App Embed Block; currently only admin publish UI exists
acceptance_criteria: Theme App Extension created and published
App Embed Block lazy-loads chat widget
Lighthouse impact < 5 points
Widget works on Online Store 2.0 themes

open_items.title: Product & catalog synchronization (initial + incremental)
open_items.description: Webhook listeners and bulk sync jobs for products, variants, collections, pages, policies
acceptance_criteria: Initial sync jobs (catalog, policies, pages) queued automatically on new shop install
ensureShopRecord detects new vs existing shop before upsert
Sync job creation failure is non-fatal — shop record returned regardless
Incremental sync via webhooks: products/create, update, delete, collections, pages, orders/paid
All webhook topics registered in shopify.server.ts
Scheduler processes PENDING jobs every 30s
Admin UI (app.data-sources.tsx) allows manual sync trigger
SyncJob status tracked in DB (PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED)
ProductProjection, PolicyProjection, OrderProjection upserted on sync
KnowledgeDocument and KnowledgeChunk created for embedding
Monorepo service packages expose shared types and contracts
10 unit tests for ensureShopRecord passing

open_items.title: Vector store upgrade to pgvector / ANN index
open_items.description: Replace current JSON-based embedding store with pgvector for production-scale retrieval
acceptance_criteria: pgvector extension enabled in PostgreSQL
EmbeddingRecord model uses vector column
ANN index (HNSW/IVFFlat) created
Retrieval latency < 100ms p99

open_items.title: External SIEM adapter integration (Datadog / Splunk)
open_items.description: Enterprise compliance SIEM export pipeline for audit events
acceptance_criteria: Audit events exported to configurable SIEM endpoint
Legal hold scoped retention exclusions per data class

open_items.title: Phase 1 E2E chat test (needs running PostgreSQL)
open_items.description: test/phase1/chat-e2e.test.ts currently fails when DB is not available
acceptance_criteria: Test passes in CI with DB available
Or test mocked to not require a live DB
open_items.evidence: Implemented in-memory Prisma mock (test/mocks/prisma-in-memory.ts). All 15 E2E tests pass. Total suite: 55/55 files, 986/986 tests.
